text: Axi-cel produced an 82% objective response rate and a 54% complete response rate.
paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
evidence_span: The objective response rate was 82%, and the complete response rate was 54%.

text: At a median follow-up of 15.4 months, 42% of patients continued to have a response.
paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
evidence_span: With a median follow-up of 15.4 months, 42% of the patients continued to have a response, with 40% continuing to have a complete response.

paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
evidence_span: The overall rate of survival at 18 months was 52%.

text: Grade 3 or higher neutropenia, anemia, and thrombocytopenia were common during treatment.
paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
evidence_span: The most common adverse events of grade 3 or higher during treatment were neutropenia (in 78% of the patients), anemia (in 43%), and thrombocytopenia (in 38%).

text: Grade 3 or higher cytokine release syndrome and neurologic events occurred in 13% and 28% of patients, respectively.
paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
outcome: cytokine release syndrome and neurologic events
evidence_span: Grade 3 or higher cytokine release syndrome and neurologic events occurred in 13% and 28% of the patients, respectively.

paper_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
evidence_span: Three of the patients died during treatment.
